originalTitle: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
title: Omar the Referee's Big Dream Trip Gets Stuck!
content:
Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here with a story that might make you feel a little bit sad. It's about a very important person named Omar Artan. Omar is from Somalia, a beautiful country right here in Africa! He loves soccer, just like many of you, and he has a very special job in the world of soccer.

Meet Omar, the Soccer Helper!

Omar Artan is what we call a "referee." Do you know what a referee does? Imagine you're playing a soccer game with your friends. Sometimes, someone needs to make sure everyone is playing fair and following the rules, right? That's what a referee does in a big, grown-up soccer game! They wear special clothes, often black, and they run all over the field, blowing a whistle and showing cards to make sure the game is fair for everyone. It's a very important job because without a good referee, a soccer game can get very messy and not fun! Omar is a really good referee from Somalia.

A Big Trip to America!

Omar had a super exciting plan! He was chosen to go all the way to a very far-off country called the United States of America. Why was he going? For something super, super big called the World Cup! The World Cup is like the biggest, most amazing soccer party in the whole wide world! Teams from all over the planet come together to play and see who is the best. It's a huge honor to be a referee at such an event. Omar was ready to help make sure the games were fair and fun for everyone watching, and for all the players too. Did you know? The United States is a very big country far away from Africa, across a huge ocean!

All the Right Papers!

Before you can visit a different country, especially one far away like the United States, you need to have special things called "papers" and a "visa." Think of "papers" like your school report card or your identity card. They tell people who you are and where you are from. A "visa" is like a special sticker or stamp in your passport that says, "Yes, this person is allowed to visit our country for a special reason, like for the World Cup!" It's like getting a special invitation. Omar worked very hard to make sure he had ALL his correct papers and his special visa. He wanted to do everything right! He checked them many times, just like you check your homework before giving it to your teacher.

A Very, Very Long Chat

So, Omar traveled all the way to the United States. He was probably feeling very excited and a little bit nervous, like when you're about to go on a big adventure! But then, something strange and sad happened. When he arrived, he had to talk to some people from the immigration office. These are the people who check everyone's papers when they come into a new country. This chat was not a quick "hello." Omar had to sit and talk with them for a super, super long time. Can you guess how long? Eleven hours! Yes, that's right, eleven hours! Imagine sitting and talking for longer than a whole school day, and even longer than when you sleep at night! That's how long Omar was there. That's a super long time for anyone, especially after a long airplane ride. Did you know? Eleven hours is longer than many kids sleep at night!

No Entry, Even With Papers

After all that talking, for eleven whole hours, guess what happened? The people told Omar that he could not come into the United States. He couldn't go to the World Cup! Even though he had all his "right papers" and his "right visa" – the special invitation – they still said no. Imagine how Omar must have felt! He had prepared everything, he had dreamed of this big trip, and he had all the correct things they asked for. It must have made him feel very sad, confused, and disappointed. It's like preparing a special drawing for a competition, making sure it's perfect, and then being told you can't even show it!

Why is this Important?

This story is important because it shows that sometimes, even when you follow all the rules and do everything right, things can still go wrong. It's not fair to Omar, who worked hard to be a good referee and to get all his travel documents ready. For Africa, it's also a little sad because Omar was going to represent Somalia and our whole continent at a huge international event. When someone from Africa gets to go to the World Cup, it makes us all proud! It shows the world the amazing talent we have here. Did you know? Somalia is a country on the East coast of Africa, known for its beautiful beaches!

What is Immigration?

Immigration is when people travel from one country to live or visit another country. The people who work in "immigration" are like very important gatekeepers. They check if you have permission to enter a country. They make sure everyone is safe and following the rules. In Omar's case, even though he had permission (his visa), something went wrong during their checks. It's a complicated system, and sometimes mistakes or problems can happen, which is very unfortunate for people like Omar.

Hopes for the Future

Even though this was a very sad event for Omar, we hope that he doesn't give up. He is still a wonderful referee, and we know he will continue to do a great job in soccer games in Africa and maybe, just maybe, he will get another chance to go to a big international tournament in the future. We can all send our good wishes to Omar Artan from Somalia! Remember, always try your best, follow the rules, and don't give up on your dreams! Did you know? Referees use a whistle to stop and start the game, and colored cards (yellow and red) to warn or send off players who break the rules!
summary: Somali referee Omar Artan, who had all his correct documents, was sadly not allowed into the United States for the World Cup after a very long interview.
whyMatters: This matters because it was unfair to Omar, who had followed all the rules, and it makes people sad when someone from Africa misses a big event that they were meant to be part of.

originalTitle: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
title: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
content: Omar Artan was set to become the first Somali to referee a game at the World Cup finals, but his place in history has been denied by US immigration authorities.

originalTitle: UN report: Israeli forces shield settlers during attacks on Palestinians
title: A Big Club Says Grown-Ups Need to Be Fair!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share an important story from far, far away, in a place called the Middle East. It might sound like a grown-up story, but it teaches us a lot about being kind and fair to everyone, just like we learn in school and at home!

Big News from a Big Club

Imagine there's a super big club in the world called the United Nations, or UN for short. This club has almost all the countries in the world as members! Their main job is to help everyone get along, solve big problems, and make sure children and grown-ups everywhere are safe and happy. They are like the principal of the whole wide world, making sure everyone follows the rules of kindness and fairness.

What the UN Saw Happening

Now, the UN has been watching something in a part of the world where two groups of people, called Israelis and Palestinians, live very close to each other. Sometimes, grown-ups from one group, who we call 'settlers,' go to places where Palestinian people live. And sometimes, these settlers do things that hurt others, like breaking things or scaring people.

Who Are the Helpers?

In this place, there are special grown-ups called Israeli security forces. You can think of them like police officers or soldiers. Their very important job is to keep everyone safe, stop bad things from happening, and make sure people follow the rules. Just like your teachers keep you safe at school, these forces are supposed to keep everyone safe in their homes and communities.

A Tricky Problem

But here's the tricky part, and what the big UN club found out: Sometimes, when these settlers were hurting Palestinian people or breaking their things, the Israeli security forces were there. And instead of stopping the settlers, the UN says that sometimes these forces would actually stand *with* the settlers. It was almost like they were making a wall, or a 'shield,' for the settlers. This meant the settlers could keep doing the bad things, and the Palestinian people didn't get the help and protection they needed. **Did you know?** The United Nations was started a long, long time ago after a big war, to help countries talk to each other instead of fighting, and to keep peace in the world!

Why is This Not Fair?

Think about it like this: Imagine you're playing in the playground, and some kids start throwing sand at others. If a teacher or a grown-up supervisor sees it, they should stop the sand-throwers and help the kids who got sand in their eyes, right? They wouldn't stand with the sand-throwers and let them keep going. That wouldn't be fair at all! This is why the UN is saying this is not fair. Everyone, everywhere, deserves to feel safe in their homes. And if someone is hurting them, the people whose job it is to protect should always step in and help the people who are being hurt, not the people causing the trouble.

What Does 'Shield' Mean Here?

When we say the forces acted as a 'shield,' it means they were standing in a way that protected the people who were causing trouble. They were making it harder for anyone to stop the bad things from happening. This made the Palestinian people feel even more scared and unsafe because the people who were supposed to protect everyone were not doing their job in a fair way. **Did you know?** Every country in the world agrees that all children have rights, like the right to be safe, to learn, and to have a home. The UN helps make sure these rights are protected for kids everywhere!

Why The UN Cares

The UN cares a lot about rules, fairness, and human rights. Human rights are like a list of important things that every single person in the world should have and be able to do, just because they are human. Things like being safe, having food, having a home, and being treated fairly are all human rights. When grown-ups don't follow these rules of fairness, the UN speaks up. They want to make sure that *all* people, no matter where they live or what they believe, can live peacefully and safely. They believe that police and soldiers should always protect *everyone* fairly and stop anyone who is hurting others.

Learning About Fairness

This story from far away teaches us a very important lesson that we can use every day. It reminds us why it's so important to be fair to everyone, to help others when they are in trouble, and to follow the rules of kindness. When we see someone being mean, it's important to speak up or get help from a grown-up who can make things fair again. **Did you know?** The UN has a special group called UNICEF that works only to help children around the world, making sure they are healthy, safe, and can go to school!

What Happens Next?

The UN is telling the world about what they saw so that the grown-ups in charge can try to make things better. They want to remind everyone that fairness is super important, especially for people whose job it is to keep everyone safe. They hope that by sharing this news, the Israeli security forces will always protect everyone fairly and stop any violence, no matter who is causing it. So, remember, friends: always be kind, always be fair, and always try to help others. These are big lessons that can make our world a happier and safer place for everyone, from your classroom to faraway lands! See you next time with more news!
summary: The United Nations, a big club of countries, reported that in a faraway land, some security forces sometimes protected grown-ups causing trouble instead of helping the people who were being hurt, which is not fair.
whyMatters: This story is important because it reminds us that everyone deserves to be safe and treated fairly, and that people whose job it is to protect should always help those who are being hurt, not those causing trouble.

originalTitle: Caution over Saka as Tuchel says England must dare to dream
title: Our Football Star Bukayo Saka Needs a Little Rest!
content:
Jambo, my little champions of Africa! Are you ready for some exciting news from the world of football? We love watching amazing players score goals and show us cool moves, don't we? Today, we're talking about a very special player named Bukayo Saka. He plays football for a team called England. And guess what? He's getting ready for a super-duper big football party called the World Cup!

Who is Bukayo Saka?

Bukayo Saka is a brilliant footballer. He is very fast and clever with the ball. He loves to run and score goals for his team. Lots of people around the world cheer for him when he plays. He is like a superhero on the football field! Did you know? Bukayo Saka is sometimes called 'Starboy' by his fans because he shines so brightly when he plays! He plays for a big team called Arsenal, but today we are talking about him playing for his country, England. When players play for their country, it's a very big honour. They want to make everyone proud.

What is the World Cup?

The World Cup is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! Imagine all your friends from different countries coming together to play football. That's what the World Cup is like, but with grown-up football players! Teams from many, many countries play against each other to see who is the best. It only happens every four years, so it's a very special event. Everyone gets excited to watch! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! It's very shiny and heavy, and every team wants to lift it up!

Saka Needs a Little Care

Now, here's the news about our friend Bukayo Saka. His coach, a very clever man named Thomas Tuchel, said that Saka's body needs a 'little bit of care' right now. What does that mean? It means that Saka might be a little bit tired, or maybe he has a tiny boo-boo that needs to heal. Like when you play outside all day and your legs feel tired, or you get a little scratch. Grown-up footballers also need to rest their bodies to stay strong. So, the coaches and doctors are making sure Saka gets enough rest and good food. They want him to be super strong and ready to run, kick, and score when the World Cup starts. They are helping him manage his body so he can play his very best! Did you know? Even the strongest footballers need to eat healthy food and get lots of sleep, just like you, to grow big and strong!

Coach Thomas Tuchel, The Team's Helper

Thomas Tuchel is like the head teacher for the England football team. He makes the plans, he teaches the players new moves, and he helps them work together. He is the one who wants Saka to be super strong for the World Cup. Coaches are very important. They help their team get ready for big games. Mr. Tuchel wants all his players to be healthy and happy so they can play amazing football. He knows that taking care of players is very important. Did you know? A good coach doesn't just teach football skills; they also help players be good teammates and never give up!

England's Big Dream

Every team that plays in the World Cup has a big dream. They all want to win the golden trophy! England is one of those teams, and they are dreaming big. They want to play wonderfully and show everyone how good they are. For Saka and his teammates, the World Cup is a chance to make their country proud. They will try their very best in every game. And to do that, they need all their players, especially amazing players like Saka, to be in top shape. Did you know? Football is called 'the beautiful game' because it brings so many people together to watch and cheer! So, let's all wish Bukayo Saka good rest and quick healing! We want to see him shine like the star he is at the World Cup. Maybe one day, you too will be playing for your country's team! Keep practicing, keep dreaming, and most importantly, keep having fun! Kwaheri, my friends!
summary: Bukayo Saka, a talented footballer for England, needs a little rest and care to be strong and ready for the exciting World Cup, as his coach Thomas Tuchel wants him to play his best.
whyMatters: This matters because we want our favourite players like Bukayo Saka to be healthy and strong so they can play amazing football for their team in big games like the World Cup. When players are well-rested, they can show us all their fantastic skills!
